div1.entryFree:
Grave, vb. (Partic. --d and --n) 1) to entomb, to bury: --d in the hollow ground, R2 III, 2, 140. ditches g. you all, Tim. IV, 3, 166.
2) to cut a little, to wound slightly, to graze: being steeled, soft sighs can never g. it, Ven. 376.
3) to engrave, to insculp, to carve: and g. upon my cheeks what helpless shame I feel, Lucr. 755. if time have any wrinkle --n there, Sonn. 100, 10. this saying --d in gold, Merch. II, 7, 36. where should be --n the slaughter of the prince, R3 IV, 4, 141 (Ff branded).
